critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:

1. BENIGN-CYCLE BIAS IN PD ESTIMATES: Training window 2010–2020 has historically low default rates. PD calibration will be optimistic relative to full-cycle performance. This is a known limitation of point-in-time credit models.

2. LOAN OFFICER SCORE AS INPUT: Introduces human judgment that may reflect individual inconsistency or systemic bias. Auditability is limited.

Key mitigant: The documentation separates base-case PD (benign) and stress PD (capital), with the stress supplement applying 2007–2009 default rate overlays. The validator's concern is addressed by this two-tier structure. Loan officer scores are industry-standard and auditable through consistency testing.

Verdict: DEFENSE_WINS.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE:

Stress supplement addresses benign-cycle concern directly. Loan officer scores are standard in commercial lending with established governance. Neither concern invalidates the model.

Verdict: DEFENSE_WINS.
adjudication_raw: Unanimous defense_wins. The stress supplement's two-tier PD approach directly responds to the benign-cycle calibration concern. Loan officer scores are standard inputs with available audit mechanisms.
